Transcript:
In this envelope is a product I've never
sold, but you know what? I'm going to
make cold calls for it. Here's how today
is going to work. I'm going to get 5
minutes to build a script on the fly,
and then I'm going to get five connects
to see if I can book a meeting. My name
is Jason Bay. I run Outbound Squad. We
train sales reps at companies like
Shopify, Zoom, Gong, Rippling, and more
on how to land meetings through the
phone. I'm going to put our training to
the test today. We're going to see if I
can land a few meetings, and hopefully
you get a few techniques that you can
use to land meetings yourself through
the phone. All right, it's time to open
the envelope. Let's see what's inside.
The Chinese side of me wants to see some
money in here.
J Bay from Titan X. Okay, cool. Let's
go, dude. So, Titan X, I'm actually
barely familiar with this company.
Essentially, their value prop is you
want your reps to make a lot of phone
calls, and people rarely pick up the
phone these days. They're going to help
us find people that have a 25, 30% plus
pick up rate, which is like six or seven
times the average. So, uh I get 5
minutes. The average cold call lasts
only 80 seconds. So, what I'm thinking
about is how am I going to make a good
first impression in the first 30?
I'm a really big fan of permission-based
openers, and I really like this idea of
a reverse pitch. So, this is the
problems. I don't really know who I'm
going to be calling yet, but I would
assume it's going to be a sales leader,
and probably someone fairly senior. So,
what I want to leverage in my PBO,
there's going to be a time ask, and
there's going to be a social proof ask.
All right, so your social proof drop,
rather. Okay, so problems. Let's just
kind of I call this chunking, chunking
up, rather. So, essentially what we want
to do is take the surface level problem
of prospects don't pick up the phone.
Um that is not a problem that an
executive cares about. That is a problem
that a rep cares about, right? So,
prospect doesn't pick up the phone,
can't land meetings,
wastes a ton of time calling
people. Okay, so if we were to chunk
this up, I'm going to assume that I'm
probably calling a director VP,
CRO
of sales, and then probably one of sales
development, I would assume. To reverse
engineer what I'm doing, I always think
about priorities. So, when you sell a
solution, one of the most common
mistakes that you can make is not
aligning to an existing priority. So,
this is something that's maybe part of
an OKR for that executive. It could be
something that they're thinking about on
a quarterly basis that there's a project
around and stuff related to pick up
rates is going to be related to pipeline
and specifically there's a couple of
trends. So, AE self-sourcing.
Um sometimes they call it AE pipe gen.
I don't know how much research I'm going
to do on the companies that I reach out
to, but there's also a moving up market.
There's also a shifting the outbound
culture.
So, it would be shifting from inbound to
outbound.
There's a whole narrative there that I
could talk about. Going to be very
similar for sales development outside of
self-sourcing. Okay, so I just like to
have some talking points. In terms of
story,
I think there's an efficiency play here
where people think about this wrong. So,
efficiency often times they think about
reduction
in head count or taking away things.
What they don't think about is how to
increase
the individual rep efficiency.
So, how to cut without cutting?
All right, that's my 5 minutes. So,
I'm ready to dial. Let's uh
let's get the list, dude. So, basically
what I'm going to do is I'm going to
kind of wing the opener a bit, but to
kind of share what's on my mind, I I'm
probably going to do something like uh
"Hey Araminta, Jason. We work with a lot
of sales teams that really struggle to
get prospects to pick up the phone like
you did just now. I'd love to share more
about how we're helping companies like
Vanta and DocuSign fix the cold calling
problem. Do you got a minute to hear
me?" And then what I'm going to do is go
in with a reverse pitch. So, I'm going
to really dig into the problem
statement. So, if it's a senior sales
leader, I'm going to say I you know, we
work with a ton of senior sales leaders
that you know, want their AEs to rely
less on SDRs and marketing for pipeline
and they need to self-source and they're
asking them to do prospecting days and
pick up the phone and dial a ton and
people just aren't doing that. I'm
curious, how does that compare to what
you guys are doing right now? What are
you seeing? And then I'll probably weave
in a customer story of some sort around
how a lot of sales leaders are thinking
about efficiency and AI and how we're
helping a lot of them you know, two to
three X the output per individual rep
without adding another tool to their
tech stack. All right, we've got the
list. Let's uh start the call counter
and let's get dialing.

to get a hold of. And these are the
people that we want our reps engaging
